versionHistory.note: Schema alignment — renamed event_user_roles references to match actual Clerk + event_user_assignments model.

People-detail response: travel is an array of legs (inbound, outbound, ground, rebookings) scoped to the URL eventId. No unique constraint on (eventId, personId) in travel_records. Aligns contract with real-world ops data entry + transport kanban + red-flag-per-leg model.

appliesTo: every event-scoped server action and API route under /events/[eventId]/* and /api/events/[eventId]/*

rules.rule: URL is the source of truth for eventId
rules.description: Server derives eventId from URL path params. Body and query-string eventId are advisory only; any mismatch is rejected.
Requests to an event the user has no role row for return HTTP 404 with a generic body. Never 403, never 200-with-empty, never disclose existence.

rules.rule: Role-based denial within an assigned event returns 403
rules.description: A user who IS a member of the event but whose role (e.g. read_only) forbids a mutation receives HTTP 403 with body {"error":"forbidden"}.
URL eventId=A + body eventId=B → HTTP 400 {"error":"eventId mismatch"}. Sentry event is emitted with {userId, urlEventId, bodyEventId, endpoint}.

errorCases.bodyPattern: must not contain eventId, event name, words 'access'/'permission'/'forbidden'

errorCases.sideEffect: Sentry event captured with {userId, urlEventId, bodyEventId, endpoint}

errorCases.note: Indistinguishable from exists-but-unmembered to prevent enumeration

errorCases.condition: Role revoked mid-session; new request to revoked event

superAdminGlobal.description: Clerk global role org:super_admin grants access to every event without requiring event_user_assignments rows
superAdminGlobal.auditRequirement: Every cross-event read/write still emits an audit log row

peopleGlobal.constraint: Per-event person attributes (registration, travel, sessions) reside in event-scoped junction tables

publicSurfaces.eventIdSource: URL or signed payload; no membership check applies
